forecaster_note: Decent morning options lining up with PM OK as well — OH surf mostly. Most breaks head to well-overhead as the SE swell keeps pumping in; S-magnets well-OH to 2x-OH-plus, sheltered corners more doable at head-high. AM clean for plenty of spots on early SW winds (best towards the southern ends), then later S'lies pull clean options back to the sheltered corners. INCOMING: a solid winter swell holds — Thu a slow fade as S/SE backs off (SW-to-S winds, AM cleanest), Fri exposures cleaning up as winds swing W (good on the back end), the weekend easing to fun offshore-morning size. — Hugh McDowell (Surfline)

wind.summary: S 15kph (23 gusts) — cross-shore on the NE beaches, straight onshore on the S-facing reefs. An early SW nudge cleans the southern ends at dawn before the S'ly fills back in; sheltered corners stay the cleanest play.

shark.summary: No shark detections at your tracked breaks (Dee Why, Long Reef, Curl Curl, Manly) or anywhere on the Northern Beaches in the past 24h — and zero active detections NSW-wide in that window. The most recent nearby sightings were ~3 days ago: drone-spotted sharks at Warriewood and Newport on 4–5 Jul (water briefly evacuated). Plenty of S-swell energy pushing bait around though — scan the lineup and check the flags.

days.label: Today
days.note: The standout morning of the week — solid SE swell (8.7ft @ 11s SE 133°) holding well overhead across the exposures. Dee Why 4–6ft, the reefs a raw 6–8ft. S wind sits cross-to-onshore, but an early SW nudge hands you a clean dawn window, best at the southern ends. Play it: Dee Why Point for frontside rights, or dawn before the S'ly fills in. 785kJ and 100/100 consistency at Dee Why means the sets keep coming — your 10–12 window should still score size with shape if the SW hangs on.

dee-why.window: Best of the four — SE 133° suits the bank and the wind sits cross-shore, not dead onshore. The Point is the pick for clean frontside rights, especially on the early SW; open bank fun but lumpier once the S'ly returns. Dawn glassiest.

tip.label: Point over beachbreak
tip.text: 785kJ and 100/100 consistency mean relentless sets, and SE 133° is a workable angle here — not the dead-S wrap that ruins it. In the S wind the Point shelters best and hands you clean frontside rights. Get on it early before the S'ly fills back in.

long-reef.window: Biggest and rawest — 1043kJ of S/SE swell straight onto the outer reefs at 2–3x overhead, but the S wind is onshore here. Experienced crew, dawn only on the early SW. Step-up / gun territory.

tip.label: Step-up or skip
tip.text: Long Reef soaks up every scrap of this swell — 1043kJ, 6–8ft — but the wind sits onshore. Big and disorganised outside the brief SW dawn window. If you paddle out, it's the FMN3 and confidence. Otherwise bookmark it for a cleaner-wind morning.

curl-curl.window: S-magnet fully loaded (805kJ) but onshore-affected. The south end / Curl Curl corner off the rocks is the cleaner nook for frontside rights, cleanest on the early SW.

tip.label: South corner or skip
tip.text: Curly eats S swell so the size is all there — 805kJ — but the open beach runs onshore and lumpy. The south-end rights off the rocks (your frontside) are the tidy option, and only really in the dawn SW window.

manly.window: Smallest and most wind-sheltered — 4–5ft on the sets with cross-shore wind. Cleaner than the S-facing reefs but softer. The mellow, rippable backup if the reefs feel too heavy.

tip.label: Sheltered but softer
tip.text: North-facing Manly (North Steyne) sees the least of this SE swell — 720kJ, 4–5ft — but it's the most wind-protected corner. Fine for a clean, performance paddle; for real size and punch the S-magnets have it, just messier.

days.label: Tomorrow
days.note: A slow fade as the S/SE swell backs off, but the trade-off is cleaner winds — SW to S, with the AM cleanest. Dee Why eases to 2–4ft, the reefs still a fun 4–5ft. This is the day for size with shape rather than raw size; your 10–12 window should be clean and rippable, dawn for the glassiest before any S'ly nudge.

dee-why.window: Cleaner morning — 2–4ft with SW-to-S winds, AM cleanest. Fun rippable session on the daily driver; the Point still holds the tidier rights. Early is best.

long-reef.window: Still real size on the outer reefs — 4–5ft with cleaner AM winds. The pick for size-with-shape; dawn before any S'ly returns.

curl-curl.window: S-magnet holds 3–4ft+ with a cleaner morning — the south corner for the more makeable walls.

manly.window: Eases to a clean 2–3ft — mellow, rippable option if the reefs feel too heavy.

days.label: Weekend
days.note: The week's big S/SE swell fades by the weekend. Saturday: SE energy eases to 2–3ft but an offshore morning window means clean, fun-size surf across the region — your kind of Saturday session. Sunday drops smaller (1–2ft) with all-day offshores, crisp and clean but groveller territory. Sat AM is the pick.

dee-why.window: Sat: clean 2–3ft in the offshore morning window — fun rippable session on the daily driver. Sun smaller (1–2ft). Punchy-beachie territory.

long-reef.window: Sat: 2–4ft of residual S energy, cleanest early. Sun eases to 1–3ft.

curl-curl.window: Sat: 2–3ft clean in the morning window — the S-magnet holds a touch more. Sun small (1–2ft+).

manly.window: Sat: mellow 2–3ft, most sheltered. Sun 1–2ft — grovel only.
